mnemonics: Accounts Are Like SAFEs (Securely Accessible, Fund-Enabled Storage): Externally Owned Accounts are like a safe you own, while Contract Accounts are like a safe with a robot arm inside that only performs programmed tasks.
keyPoints: There are two types of accounts: Externally Owned Accounts (EOAs), controlled by a private key, and Contract Accounts (CAs), controlled by their code.
Every account has a balance in Ether (ETH), which can be sent and received.
Accounts are the fundamental entities that interact on the blockchain; transactions are state changes between accounts.
commonMistakes: Assuming smart contracts are the only type of account (they are not; EOAs are user-controlled accounts).
Believing that contract accounts have a private key (they are controlled solely by their code logic).
